text: patches and neat, tight stitches. The only unrepaired damage were the three small holes on its chest. Its pack was slung over one shoulder, and she assumed its rifle was over the other, bundled in a protective bag. Her bravado would have burned out a lot faster if she’d noticed that while she brandished her knife earlier . The bot’s boots were well-worn, but had been meticulously cared for — unlike her own. She couldn’t bear to look back at her footwear, knowing that one boot lay on its side, sole hanging open in something too close to a frown . “Why do you keep these things?” the bot asked . “What are you talking about ?” “They serve no purpose .” “I thought they were pretty,” she said defensively, looking over her collection . It reminded her of what she’d found earlier. Folding the remaining bit of meat into her mouth, she opened her bag and removed the frame. Fortunately, though the bag itself was damp, the photo hadn’t gotten wet. As she carried the frame toward the shelves, she noticed the bot holding the porcelain shard between its fingers, brow furrowed as it examined the item. The bot seemed… confused . Lara placed the photo on the top shelf, leaning the frame against the wall. Stepping back, she observed it as a piece of her larger collection, beautiful despite the damage the frame had taken . “That world is lost to all of us,” the bot said . “No shit,” Lara replied, clenching her jaw so she didn’t show how deeply his words affected her. Just another reminder of how desolate her life was. This here — this lonely shack — was as good as things would ever be. “Say what you wanted to say so you can get out of here .” The bot was quiet, staring at the photo for what felt like years. Its body was too close to hers, too unmoving; no rise and fall of its chest, no shifting of its feet. It was unnatural . “You need food. Clean water. Reliable shelter,” it finally said. She intended to reply with another no shit , but the bot’s next words stole her breath. “I can give you all that .” “Just give it?” she asked when she regained her composure. “Just like that? Nothing is free .” “You’re right. Forgive my choice of words. It would be an arrangement .” “Look, I don’t know how fried your processor got out in the Dust, but I’ve already told you, I don’t — ” The bot lifted its hand, holding up a single finger. Lara snapped her mouth shut, glaring at him . “Our current arrangement is that you will listen,” it said, meeting her gaze. Its eyes were so impossibly green. “I will provide food, water, shelter, clothing — any and all of the things your body requires to function. You will quarter at my residence—” it leveled its finger at her, silencing the protest she was about to make, “—for the duration of this agreement. The price is simple, and one you can easily pay .” “No.” She didn’t care about their arrangement, didn’t want an agreement . There was no way she’d become a bot’s plaything. The very thought sent a shiver along her spine, threatening to stir up memories of cruel, inhuman hands on her body . The bot was silent, staring at her with expressionless eyes as her heart thumped . “Dance for me,” it finally said. “That’s the only payment I ask .” “God dammit, I said I don’t do that anymore! Go to Kitty’s .” “I did. It was… unsatisfying .” Lara’s brows fell. Unsatisfying? There were bot and human dancers there, all beautiful. They were seductive, sensual, and — unlike Lara — offered private couplings for a price. How could a place like that not satisfy whatever cravings this thing had ? She paused, thinking over its words again. The more she thought about them, the more human they seemed . “What do you mean ?” “I went and watched. None of them danced like you .” “So, you just decided — after seeing me dance for a few seconds — that you want me as your own, personal little dancer in your house ?” “ Yes .” She waited for it to say more, to elaborate, but it seemed to think that one word was enough . Lara lifted a leg to scratch at the dried mud on her calf. Despite the meat she’d wolfed down, hunger gnawed at the pit of her stomach. This bot was offering everything she could ever need, the first comfort of her entire life, and she was tempted to refuse again . Tabitha had worked double shifts after Lara left Kitty’s, just to keep the two of them fed. Even knowing what had happened to Lara, Tabitha still succumbed to a similar offer from another bot. Warmth and a full belly beat having perverts pawing at your tits and ass every night . “What’s your name?” the bot asked . She pushed aside the guilt that always accompanied thoughts of Tabitha . “ Lara .” “Is that all ?” “What the hell do you mean, is that all ?” “Humans usually have two or three names. Is it just Lara ?” “Oh. Brooks. Lara Brooks.” She tugged at the wet fabric around her neck, loosening it. “What’s… yours ?” “I told you yesterday.” Was there a hint of offense in its tone? That wasn’t possible; bots didn’t work that way . “I was a little scared, okay? I wasn’t really paying attention to names and all .” “Do most humans threaten violence when they’re a little scared ?” “Yes, actually,” she replied, folding her arms across her chest. Especially the ones who’ve had bad experiences with bots. “So, you gonna tell me your name again or not ?” It tilted its head, never taking its eyes off her. Dread balled
